articles.title: Facing Indus Waters Treaty Fallout, Bilawal Bhutto Threatens India In Pakistan Parliament: 'We'll Retaliate'

articles.title: 'TVK a democratic revolution’: After 55 years in CPI, veteran leader Mahendran joins Vijay’s party
articles.description: Veteran CPI leader C Mahendran has joined actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, citing a desire to strengthen communist ideology and public welfare. Mahendran, a seasoned politician with a five-decade record, believes TVK offers a platform for ordinary citizens and aims to eradicate corruption. He envisions the party as a mass movement, marking a significant shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: Pakistan PM's visit to Switzerland postponed due to electronic signing of US-Iran deal: Spokesman
articles.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has postponed a visit to Switzerland for a US-on Thursday. "The proposed visit has been postponed as the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has already been electronically signed, has entered into force, and is now under implementation," spokesman Mosharraf Zaidi said, adding Pakistan would support the next phase of several "technical-level" tracks. bur-ceg/lga

articles.title: PM-Kisan 23rd instalment on June 20: Rs 18,880 crore to be transferred to 9.44 crore farmers
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will release the 23rd instalment of the PM-Kisan scheme on June 20 in West Bengal, transferring Rs 18,880 crore to over 9.44 crore farmers. He will also launch crop insurance schemes, an Agri-Tech platform, and the National Natural Farming Mission, alongside inaugurating rural road projects.

articles.title: UP TGT result expected to be released soon at upessc.up.gov.in: Check steps to download scorecards
articles.description: The Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission (UPESSC) is expected to announce the UP TGT Result 2026 soon on its official website. The result will determine the selection status of thousands of candidates who appeared for the recruitment examination. Along with the scorecards, the commission will release the merit list and category-wise cut-off marks.

articles.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old monument in Wiltshire linked to early Stonehenge traditions
articles.description: A remarkable 5,000-year-old wooden monument, predating Stonehenge by 500 years, has been unearthed near Bulford, Wiltshire. This discovery, featuring two large posts aligned with the midsummer sunrise and midwinter sunset, suggests sophisticated ancient astronomy and ceremonial traditions were already established. Researchers believe this site may have inspired the later construction of Stonehenge, offering crucial insights into Neolithic communities.

articles.title: Amid TMC, Shiv Sena (UBT) rebellions, the numbers that could benefit NDA in Parliament
articles.description: The NDA currently holds 293 seats in the 540-member Lok Sabha. A two-thirds majority would require support from around 360 members.

articles.title: Is The Worst Over For Dalal Street? Expert Warns Markets May Be Getting Too Complacent
articles.description: Market sentiment has improved significantly in recent weeks, with equities rebounding sharply and global concerns easing. Optimism surrounding geopolitical developments, stabilizing energy prices, and a strong recovery in major markets such as the US has boosted investor confidence. In India, benchmark indices have recovered much of their earlier losses, while pressure on the rupee and inflation concerns have moderated. According to Prateek Nigudkar, Senior Fund Manager, Shriram AMC, investors should welcome the improving outlook but remain cautious, as several risks continue to linger beneath the surface. While RBI measures have helped stabilize financial markets and support the currency, uncertainty around global growth and market valuations remains. Here's why experts believe the outlook has improved, but the markets may still not be completely out of the woods.

The conflict in Ukraine continues with no diplomatic resolution in sight.
